laws.title: Pennsylvania Minimum Wage — $7.25/hr (Federal Rate)
laws.summary: Pennsylvania's minimum wage equals the federal minimum of $7.25 per hour. There is no state-level increase above the federal rate currently in effect. Tipped employees may receive $2.83/hour from the employer, provided tips bring total compensation to at least $7.25/hour.
compliance_actions: Pay all non-exempt employees at least $7.25/hour
For tipped employees, pay at least $2.83/hour and ensure tips bring total compensation to $7.25/hour
Post the Pennsylvania Minimum Wage Law poster in a conspicuous workplace location
Retain payroll records documenting hours worked and wages paid for at least 3 years
